target.name: Zettascale
target.founder: Elias Almqvist (CEO; self-taught engineer, Chalmers University CS/CE dropout at 21), Prithvi Raj (CTO; MEng Computational Stats + ML Cambridge; ex-Microsoft)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 23.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Zettascale is a seed-stage semiconductor company founded by Elias Almqvist (CEO) and Prithvi Raj (CTO), with $3M+ raised to date. The chips and semiconductors sector contains 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers, including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, and Etched.

The valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method: stage-progression analysis. Using a $10.0B total-raised proxy as a starting point, a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied value of $25.0B. Public-comp anchoring proved inapplicable due to lack of disclosed revenue data, and peer-median triangulation was infeasible given insufficient narrow-set peers meeting same-stage or similar-tier filters (fewer than 3 datapoints across tighter cohorts).

The analysis supports a range of $21.2B (low) to $28.7B (high), with a median of $25.0B. This valuation carries material uncertainty. The stage-progression method is the sole triangulation input, creating dependency on the reasonableness of both the $10.0B proxy and the 2.5x multiplier for seed progression in the frontier-chip subsector. Public multiples (23.4x P/S) remain available as future anchor points pending revenue disclosure. Key risks include technology execution risk inherent to early-stage chip ventures and thinness of true comparability within the seed-stage frontier-semiconductor cohort.
